stage: Stage 1: Data Loading, Initial Profiling, and Cleaning

data_files.description: Initial cleaned dataset with missing values handled
Final cleaned dataset with income sentinel values fixed

results_files.description: Conversion rates by categorical feature values
Numerical feature statistics by purchase status

visualization_files.description: 4-panel analysis: variant ranking, conversion rates, coinsurance/deductible effects
6-panel analysis: age distributions, pet demographics, geographic patterns

key_insights: Current production variant (80_250_20000) ranks 14/27 - significant improvement opportunity
Best variant (70_100_50000) achieves $111.90/quote vs current $98.39/quote (13.7% improvement)
Paradoxical finding: Lower coinsurance (70%) outperforms higher coinsurance (90%)
Overall conversion rate is 15.08% with 52.5% performance difference between best and worst variants
131 sentinel values (-666,666,666) found in income data and properly imputed
All 27 expected variants present with balanced distribution (~2,000-2,200 per variant)
4 forward-looking features identified and excluded to prevent data leakage
High cardinality in pet breeds (399 unique) will require feature engineering
recommendations_for_next_stages: Stage 2: Deep dive into variant performance patterns and parameter interactions
Stage 3: Comprehensive feature analysis including temporal patterns and geographic variations
Stage 4: Feature engineering for high cardinality features and interaction terms
Stage 5: Synthesize findings and create modeling roadmap
